reasoning: Starting with A=10, B=5, Y=15. I need to determine how Y depends on A and B. I will first increase A to see if Y changes, which will help identify the relationship.

From the history, increasing A from 10 to 20 (doubled) while keeping B constant at 5 caused Y to increase from 15 to 25 (an increase of 10). This suggests a linear relationship where Y = m*A + c. If Y = A + B, then 10+5=15 and 20+5=25, which matches perfectly. I will test this hypothesis by changing B to see if Y changes by the same amount as B.

Based on the history, when A doubled from 10 to 20 (with B=5), Y increased from 15 to 25. This fits Y = A + B perfectly (10+5=15, 20+5=25). In the current state, A=20, B=10, and Y=30. This also fits Y = A + B (20+10=30). The relationship is consistently additive. I have sufficient evidence to conclude the equation is Y = A + B.
